The Reality

They Defended
Our Freedom.

Over one million Ukrainians have taken up arms since Russia's full-scale invasion. When the fighting pauses, a different battle begins — one fought inside the mind, at home, alone.

"
"Never in my life did I think I would learn to ski. This is the first time in two years that I haven't heard the daily sounds of war. I am grateful for this experience."
— Ihor, Ukrainian Action Hero & Combat Veteran

Our Model

A Holistic Approach to Recovery

Hosted at the Sunrise Rehabilitation Center in Bulgaria — one of the largest integrated wellness facilities in Eastern Europe — VBI delivers structured two-week rehabilitation retreats for cohorts of twenty soldiers and family members.

Our clinical partnership with Ukrainian Action Heroes (UAH) — which has already served 80+ veterans in the Carpathian Mountains — brings proven, culturally-grounded therapeutic methods into every cohort.

A cornerstone of our model is our partnership with U.S. veterans organizations, placing American combat veterans alongside Ukrainian soldiers as mentors — those who have walked the same path and found a way forward. As we believe: "Recovery begins when purpose returns."

  • All-Inclusive Care — therapy, accommodation, meals, transport, and wellness fully covered
  • Family-Focused Recovery — spouses and children participate to prevent intergenerational trauma
  • Integrated Therapy — physical rehabilitation, mindfulness, counseling, and creative therapy
Arrive & Ground

Veterans and families arrive at Sunrise Center and begin stabilization through mindfulness and breathwork.

01
Heal Together

Structured therapy, physiotherapy, and group sessions help individuals and families process trauma safely.

02
Find Purpose

U.S. veteran mentors join cohorts, sharing lived experience and modeling resilience through service.

03
Return as Leaders

Graduates return to their communities equipped to rebuild and inspire others.

04

The Family

When One Heals,
A Family Heals.

Trauma doesn't stop at the veteran. It travels home — to spouses, children, generations. VBI includes families in every cohort, breaking the cycle before it begins.

Mind & Body

Movement as Medicine.
Mindfulness as Armor.

Each day at VBI begins and ends with movement — yoga, breathwork, and mindfulness sessions that rebuild the connection between body and mind that combat severs.

Purpose Through Mentorship

Purpose Through Mentorship

At the heart of VBI's model is a profound truth: healing is most powerful when it serves a purpose. Each cohort is joined by U.S. veterans who serve as mentors — sharing experience, resilience, and hard-won lessons in overcoming trauma.

Ukrainian soldiers gain clinical recovery and the guidance of those who truly understand their path. U.S. veteran mentors rediscover renewed meaning through service to others — a living cycle of compassion, leadership, and hope that only veterans can create for each other.

Veterans who once served on different continents now serve together in healing. Service never ends. It simply evolves.

Strategic Partners
Ukrainian Action Heroes Hard Rock Charlie Feherty's Troops First Foundation Sunrise Rehabilitation Center
100s+
Veterans Served by UAH

Ukrainian Action Heroes has already proven the model — VBI now scales it with the Sunrise Center's world-class facilities.

14 Days
Immersive Program

No distractions. No gaps. Two full weeks of structured, residential healing — clinically supervised from arrival to departure.

$500K+
Lifetime Value Per Veteran

A rehabilitated veteran generates $500K+ in lifetime value vs. $1M+ in untreated costs — a return that transforms families and nations.

Your Support. Real Impact.

Sponsor a Soldier

$175/day
Turn-Key Per Participant

Just $175 a day covers everything — travel, world-class therapists, accommodation, meals, wellness programming, and mentorship.

Broken Cycles
Intergenerational Impact

Recovery prevents secondary trauma in spouses and children — breaking the cycle of generational impact and restoring families as the foundations of resilient communities.

Renewed Purpose
For U.S. Veteran Mentors

Every American veteran who participates gains renewed meaning and connection. Healing is reciprocal — and service, for these heroes, never truly ends.

Veterans who once fought alone now recover together — across nations, across generations, united by shared sacrifice and shared hope.
